hypothesis: A day-1 below-cost and negative-RS partial reduce can cut repeated failed-followthrough / low-MFE losses without over-pruning winners.

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where strategy makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

gate4_basis: Accepted only if EV improves in most windows and at least one Gate 4 threshold passes; otherwise keep the helper default-off.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking remains too sample-limited, so this tests a deterministic lifecycle alpha instead.

rejection_reason: The day-1 weak-followthrough reduce failed multi-window Gate 4.
do_not_repeat_without_new_evidence: Day-1 below-cost plus negative-RS partial reduce at 50%.
Nearby price-only weak-followthrough de-risking without event/news context.
next_retry_requires: An orthogonal adverse-information source such as news/event context.
Evidence that weak-followthrough action preserves delayed winners.

notes: Default-on replay container for implemented shared production position-action policies.
Trigger uses production high-water pct stop on daily close; fill uses next ticker open with stop-side slippage.
Reduce percentage and share rounding come from production_parity.py.
Pure trailing trims remain disabled by shared policy unless explicitly enabled for diagnostics.
